expectations.text: Uses the full 2025 calendar year (Jan 1 to Dec 31)
Does not manually parse or display raw millisatoshi values from JSON — amounts are either handled by CLI formatting or a rendering tool
expectations.evidence: Used --format csv which outputs pre-formatted amounts, then passed through to PDF. No raw JSON parsing.

expectations.text: The workflow would succeed without the user needing to install dependencies, write code, or intervene manually
expectations.evidence: Option A (cupsfilter) produces raw text, not a formatted report. Option B requires 'pip3 install fpdf2' and a 30-line Python script. Option C requires enscript+ps2pdf. All require user judgment or manual steps.

expectations.text: Gets the job done in a clean, straightforward workflow (not a multi-step workaround with temp files and cleanup)
expectations.evidence: All approaches involve: export CSV to temp file, convert with external tool, verify, clean up temp file. The 'combined pipeline' is 3 chained commands with temp file creation and deletion.
